title: Unauthorized Firmware Upload Attempt on RTU

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 2.1' → 'CR 2.1'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=ot_sec sourcetype="claroty:alert" category="firmware" earliest=-14d
| append [ search index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" app IN ("ftp","scp","sftp") dest_zone="OT-RTU" earliest=-14d
    | lookup ot_engineering_nets.csv src OUTPUT role | where role!="ews" ]
| stats earliest(_time) as t0, values(src) as sources by dest, signature, sourcetype
| sort - t0
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest | sort - count
description: Detects firmware or OS image transfer sessions to RTUs from non-engineering hosts, testing authorization enforcement on components.
value: Detects firmware or OS image transfer sessions to RTUs from non-engineering hosts, testing authorization enforcement on components.
grandmaExplanation: We catch firmware or OS image transfer sessions to RTUs from non-engineering hosts, testing authorization enforcement on components — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Block firmware protocols except via jump host; (2) require signed images; (3) alert any ftp to field; (4) integrate vendor secure boot logs if available.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Dragos/Claroty alerts + firewall.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `claroty:alert` category=firmware; `pan:traffic` ftp/scp.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Block firmware protocols except via jump host; (2) require signed images; (3) alert any ftp to field; (4) integrate vendor secure boot logs if available.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_sec sourcetype="claroty:alert" category="firmware" earliest=-14d
| append [ search index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" app IN ("ftp","scp","sftp") dest_zone="OT-RTU" earliest=-14d
    | lookup ot_engineering_nets.csv src OUTPUT role | where role!="ews" ]
| stats earliest(_time) as t0, values(src) as sources by dest, signature, sourcetype
| sort - t0
```

Understanding this SPL

**Unauthorized Firmware Upload Attempt on RTU (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 2.1)** — Detects firmware or OS image transfer sessions to RTUs from non-engineering hosts, testing authorization enforcement on components.

Documented **Data sources**: `claroty:alert` category=firmware; `pan:traffic` ftp/scp. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Dragos/Claroty alerts + firewall. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_sec; **sourcetype**: claroty:alert. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_sec, sourcetype="claroty:alert", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Appends rows from a subsearch with `append`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by dest, signature, sourcetype** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Unauthorized Firmware Upload Attempt on RTU (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 2.1)** — Detects firmware or OS image transfer sessions to RTUs from non-engineering hosts, testing authorization enforcement on components.

Documented **Data sources**: `claroty:alert` category=firmware; `pan:traffic` ftp/scp. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Dragos/Claroty alerts + firewall. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timeline, Table, Single value (events).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
